easy.en: Improvising off of a simple melody.

light.en: Improvising from a common major chord progression.

medium.en: Improvising off of a common melody.

hard.en: Improvising from a known chord progression ( e.g., minor, blues, etc.).

very_hard.en: Improvising off of a melody just heard ( used in dueling instruments).

extremely_hard.en: Improvising from an unfamiliar chord progression.

sheer_folly.en: Improvising a new chord progression.

absurd.en: Improvising a complete work.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for improvising or reading poetry. It may also be used to recognize poetical styles, forms, etc. Note that a minimum of 4 ranks in the spoken language utilized is required to use this skill.
skills.notes_en: This skill is used by bards and historians to entertain royal courts, by Elves to praise one another, and by romantics wooing their lovers. The difficulty of the maneuver depends on the length of the poem being recited, or improvised. When reciting poetry, a strong meter and rhyme pattern reduces the difficulty by one level. When improvising, attempting to maintain both meter and rhyme increases the difficulty by one level. Each rank of poetic improvisation indicates knowledge of one epic poem or equivalent amount of shorter material. Master bards can recite any number of epic poems.
specialties: Any specific type of poem (e.g., humorous, epic, tragic, etc.)

routine.en: A limerick.

easy.en: A sonnet or short series of heroic couplets.

light.en: A short poem (2 or 3 verses).

medium.en: An average length poem ( 4 to 6 verses, plus a possible chorus).

hard.en: A long poem (7 or more verses).

very_hard.en: A (relatively) short epic.

extremely_hard.en: A full epic.

absurd.en: Improvising an epic.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for the writing of poetry, or any highly stylized form of writing. Note that the individual must have at least 4 ranks in the written language to be used. Note that this form of poetry is typically of a significantly greater quality than improvisational poetry.
skills.notes_en: This skill is used to write a poem. The difficulty of the maneuver depends on the intricacy of the poem's structure (meter and rhyme) and depth of emotion.
specialties: Any specific type of poem ( e.g., humorous, epic, tragic, etc.)

routine.en: A slogan or jingle that rhymes. No self-respecting poet would ever write many of these.

easy.en: A brief open-form poem that describes some subject without emotion.

light.en: A short, structured poem that approaches its subject in an innovative manner.

medium.en: An open-form poem that invokes a simple emotional response (e.g., sadness or laughter).

hard.en: A perfectly metered and rhymed poem that inspires a simple emotional response, perhaps in an innovative manner.

very_hard.en: A long poem that touches on a number of emotional responses.

extremely_hard.en: A complete collection that honestly addresses a logical series of emotions (e.g., the author's journey from boy to man).

sheer_folly.en: An epic poem that causes the reader to identify strongly with the hero, feeling his pain and sharing in his triumph.

absurd.en: A masterpiece that allows the reader temporarily to experience the divine.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for recognizing and identifying poisons, as well as knowledge of proper application and usage techniques. (These may require a Using/ Removing Poison skill roll to actually carry through.)
skills.notes_en: The difficulty of locating a poison is indicated in RMFRP (p. 149). That provides the difficulty of finding such a poison in the wild. The number associated with the difficulty of finding a given poison indicates its rarity. If a character has 5 ranks in Poison Lore (i.e., knowledge level 4), he is familiar with jitsu (m-O-4), including its specific effects. He knows that zaganzar (t· M-6) can be found in temperate mountains and that silmaana (s-0.2) is a level 9 poison with no addiction factor.

routine.en: Knowledge of category ( e.g., circulatory, respiratory, etc.).

easy.en: Knowledge of climate/locale codes (e.g., m-U).

light.en: Knowledge of form/preparation (e.g., root/paste).

medium.en: Knowledge of specific effects (e.g., delivers 1- 100 hits).

hard.en: Knowledge of market cost and antidotes.

very_hard.en: Knowledge of addiction factor and level.

extremely_hard.en: Identifying a poison in its prepared state.

sheer_folly.en: Knowledge of the combined effects of two poisons in the same category.

absurd.en: Knowledge of the combined effects of two poisons in different categories.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for perceiving the presence of poison in or on an item or object. Note that some poisons will simply be undetectable (e.g., a colorless, tasteless, odorless powder that dis- . solves completely in water will be very difficult if not '&~~ ~· impossible to detect). '
skills.notes_en: The difficulty of detecting a poison depends on the method of application (pastes are usually obvious) and the number of senses that can detect the poison. For example, cyanide has a distinctive odor, although if mixed with some food that masks the poison, it is more difficult to detect. The base difficulties assume that the poison can be seen, smelled and tasted. For each sense removed, add one to the difficulty. For example, iocaine is a powder that is colorless, odorless and tasteless. It is an Absurd maneuver to detect this poison. Sharkasar is a paste that has an odor and is dark brown. Since it has no taste, it is a Light maneuver to detect this poison.

easy.en: Paste.

medium.en: Liquid.

very_hard.en: Powder.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for handling a pole to surmount or cross over an obstacle.
skills.notes_en: Use of this skill assumes a sprint the round before. For each pace category above or below, the difficulty is increased by one. If the character does not have a proper pole (flexible, yet strong), the difficulty is also increased by one.

routine.en: 4' high or 4' wide.

easy.en: 6' high or 6' wide.

light.en: 8 1 high or 8' wide.

medium.en: 10' high or 10' wide.

hard.en: 12' high or 15' wide.

very_hard.en: 15' high or 20' wid1

extremely_hard.en: 18' high or 30

sheer_folly.en: 22' high or 40' wk.~.

absurd.en: 25' high or 50' wide.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for seeing active Essence (blue), Channeling (red) and Mentalism (green) magic. If the GM allows players to develop the skill of Power Perception he should stipulate that Detect Magic spells will detect magic, active or inactive {e.g., waiting spells).
skills.notes_en: The difficulty of this maneuver depends on the power of the magic. Extremely powerful dweomers (enchantments) may be so overwhelming that it is difficult not to notice the presence of the spell. The distance from the location (center) of the spell, diminishes one's ability to detect the spell. Note that this skill reveals nothing about the level of the sSpell or effect.

routine.en: Deific magic.

easy.en: 50th level or higher spell/ effect.

light.en: 37th - 49th level spell/ effect.

medium.en: 26th • 36th level spell/effect.

hard.en: 17th - 25th level spell/ effect.

very_hard.en: 10th • 16th level spell/ effect.

extremely_hard.en: 5th • 9th level spell/ effect.

sheer_folly.en: 2nd• 4th level spell/effect.

absurd.en: 1st level spell/effect.

skills.description_en: A character's total Power Points (PP) is equal to his Power Point Development skill bonus. This skill allows a sSpell user to increase the total number of Power Points he may hold at any given time. The skill bonus progression for this skill is found the chart on page 85 of RMFRP. A hybrid sSpell caster averages his realm stat bonuses to obtain his stat bonus for this skill.

This skill allows the use of a controlled burst of strength to increase the force of a blow, such as with a sword, hammer, or fist. The use of this skill requires a 40% activity action the round before the strike takes place. The round of the strike, the character receives a bonus to his OB according to how much force he puts into the blow, which in turn affects the difficulty of the maneuver.
skills.notes_en: The difficulty of the maneuver depends only on the amount of extra OB sought.

routine.en: +1 OB modification.

easy.en: +4 OB modification.

light.en: +7 OB modification.

medium.en: +10 OB modification.

hard.en: +20 OB modification.

very_hard.en: +30 OB modification.

extremely_hard.en: +40 OB modification.

sheer_folly.en: +50 OB modification.

absurd.en: +60 OB modification.

skills.description_en: The skill allows the use of a controlled burst of strength to increase the range of a thrown object, such as a javelin, hammer, stone, etc. The use of this skill requires a 40% activity action the round before the throw takes place. The round of the throw, the character receives a bonus to his weapon range according to how much force he places into the throw, which in turn affects the difficulty of the maneuver.
skills.notes_en: If a large item is thrown as a weapon, the attack is resolved as a Fall/Crush attack (see RMFRP p. 226 or AL p. 75) with an OB of +1/10 pounds. The range modifier depends on the difficulty of the Power-throwing maneuver. The maximum distance an object can be thrown is 1 foot for every 2 unused pounds of lifting capacity (see Weight-lifting).

routine.en: -5 OB / foot.

easy.en: -3 OB / foot.

light.en: -2 OB / foot.

medium.en: -1 OB/ foot+10% range.

hard.en: -1 OB/ 2 feet+25% range.

very_hard.en: -1 OB / 3 feet+50% range.

extremely_hard.en: -1 OB/ 5 feet+75% range.

sheer_folly.en: -1 OB/ 10 feet+100% range.

absurd.en: -1 OB/ 20 feet+125% range.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus to successfully prepare a medicinal herb from its natural state into a usable form.
skills.notes_en: Use of this skill supposes that the herbalist knows how the herb must be applied. The difficulty of the maneuver depends on how many ingredients are needed to purify the herb. As a general rule, divide the cost of the herb (in gold) by the rarity factor squared (cost .,.. [rarity x rarity]) and reference below. (The GM may declare that some herbs are more or less difficult than this.)
specialties: Any specific method of preparation ( e.g., brew, ingest, apply)

routine.en: Below .01.

absurd.en: 10 or more.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus to successfully prepare a poison from its natural state into a usable form.
skills.notes_en: Use of this skill supposes that the herbalist knows how the poison must be applied. The difficulty of the maneuver depends on how many ingredients are needed to prepare the poison. As a general rule, divide the cost of the poison (in gold) by the rarity factor squared (cost .,.. [rarity x rarity]) and reference below. (The GM may declare that some poisons are more or less difficult than this.)
specialties: Any specific method of preparation ( e.g .. brew

routine.en: Below .01.

very_hard.en: 2 - 4. Ext. Hard: 4 - 7.

absurd.en: 10 or more.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for influencing others through indirect means, rather than direct speech. Includes such ideas as starting rumors, as well as designing large advertising campaigns.
skills.notes_en: Propaganda is used to influence public opinion. It can be used to lower the public's opinion of their leader, justify a war, or anything a clever manipulator can dream of. Unlike advertising, this skill is not trying to sell a product. The effects of propaganda are more ethereal than profit (although the two are certainly related). The difficulty of the attempt depends on how much of a change is being attempted. This assumes, naturally, that the allegation, claim, or assertion is plausible. The more outrageous the statement, the more difficult it's going to be to sell it to the public. In general, successful use of this skill will modify public opinion by up to 5%. The following examples assume that a small number of people are being influenced, fewer than 100. For each factor of 100, the difficulty should be increased by one. Hence, a Propaganda maneuver intended to affect a country of 1,000,000 inhabitants would be increased by two levels of difficulty.
specialties: Any type of claim (political, religious, etc.)

routine.en: A claim that is very reasonable. For example, Aeron is known for his sexual exploits. It is not difficult to convince the public that he is having an affair.

easy.en: A claim that is truthful, but sounds implausible. Continuing the above example, it would be an Easy maneuver to convince the public that the allegations of impropriety are wrong.

light.en: A claim that reinterprets the truth. Spin doctors attempt such maneuvers to re-direct public attention to a different aspect of a situation.

medium.en: A claim whose assertions cannot be verified. For example, there is a vast government conspiracy that includes the disappearance of its citizens. The very nature of the claim prohibits verification.

hard.en: A claim that is contrary to the public's morals or beliefs. Convincing a tolerant public that Ores are pondscum would be a Hard maneuver.

very_hard.en: A claim that requires the public to re-examine some core religious or cultural belief. The Protestant Reformation would be one example.

extremely_hard.en: Inspiring the public to take action about some concern that is relevant to them and not contrary to their beliefs. For example, a campaign to convince more people to bathe regularly.

sheer_folly.en: Convincing the public to stop performing some mundane activity, including a successful product ban.

absurd.en: Inspiring the public to take some action that goes against their core beliefs. Inciting a rebellion through propaganda alone, for example. (This would be much easier if a strong leader were to emerge.)

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for recognizing and identifying major insanities (including manias, phobias, depressions, dementia, etc.) and attempting to heal or correct them. It must be developed either in-depth for a spefl"-,~ cific race or in ..._ ___________ _ general trends of multi-cultural racial makeups. Several races may be developed simultaneously provided research materials are available. See Gamemaster Law for more information on psychological disorders and their effects.
skills.notes_en: See Healing, Section 4.0.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for impressing, entertaining, or manipulating groups of people directly. Note that this is different from the Tale Telling skill, which is used on small groups, using stories and fables for entertainment and instruction. Public Speaking is. for larger groups, is often more extemporaneous, and is used to manipulate the emotions or views of a crowd.
skills.notes_en: This skill is used to directly influence an audience (unlike Propaganda, which is used to indirectly influence the public). The difficulty of the attempt depends on how much attention the audience is paying, and how radical the recommendations of the speaker. In general, a Public Speaking maneuver will have direct effects on 10% of the audience, and indirect effects on the entire audience.
specialties: Any specific group (e.g., sailors, soldiers, etc.)
Any specific message (e.g., repentance, glory, etc.)

routine.en: The message reinforces common and stronglyheld beliefs (e.g., a rallying speech prior to combat).

easy.en: The audience worships the speaker, hanging on his every word (e.g., a beloved regent).

light.en: The message reminds the audience of common beliefs that have been temporarily forgotten. This includes regaining morale following a defeat or reminding a congregation of the importance of charity.

medium.en: The audience is interested in what the speaker has to say. They will probably agree based solely off of their impression of the speaker ( e.g., a hero returning to his homeland).

hard.en: The message modifies a common belief, but does not directly contradict it. For example, reminding people that a free and just society can harshly punish those who threaten freedom and justice.

very_hard.en: The audience is bored or disinterested. Ext. Hard: The message is contrary to the audience's beliefs. Convincing the audience that a criminal should be released, or that they should voluntarily pay taxes.

sheer_folly.en: The audience is trying to ignore the speaker. Most street prophets must endure hostile audiences.

absurd.en: Converting people from one religion to another.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for getting a weapon from a scabbard into battle very quickly. If successful, the weapon is out of the scabbard and in hand and moreover may make an attack and/or move the same round without the normal 20% activity required (-20 penalty) for drawing a weapon. If the skill check fails, he has dropped the weapon. On a Partial Success, the character takes the normal time (and penalty) to draw the weapon.
skills.notes_en: This skill is nearly identical to Adrenal Quickdraw. If this skill is performed successfully, a weapon can be both drawn and used during the snap action phase (since drawing the weapon is a 0% action). If used with a thrown weapon, it is possible to throw two weapons in one round (provided one is already in hand). The GM may even wish to allow this skill to be used with missile weapons (representing Zen archery). The difficulty of the skill is based entirely on the size of the weapon.

routine.en: Readying a thrown weapon.

easy.en: Readying a one-handed edged weapon.

light.en: Readying a one-handed crushing weapon.

medium.en: Readying a two-handed weapon.

hard.en: Readying two weapons (both are one-handed edged).

very_hard.en: Readying two weapons.

extremely_hard.en: Readying a short bow or sling.

sheer_folly.en: Readying a composite bow or long bow.

absurd.en: Readying any other weapon (subject to GM approval).

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for rapidly descending from a height, using ropes and other equipment. If the Rappelling maneuver is unsuccessful, the individual has a chance of falling. Rappelling rate is equal to the individual's normal movement rate, with modifiers due to speed.
skills.notes_en: The difficulty of a Rappelling maneuver depends on the quality of the surface being descended. Uneven surfaces with multiple protrusions are more difficult to rappel down than smooth brick walls.

condition_modifiers.condition_en: No rope or equipment (use Climbing or Scaling skill)

routine.en: Smooth brick or concrete wall at a 75 ° angle.

easy.en: Smooth brick or concrete wall.

light.en: Rough brick wall.

medium.en: Smooth cliff face with no extreme protrusion.

hard.en: An uneven cliff face with some protrusions.

very_hard.en: An extremely uneven surface.

extremely_hard.en: An extremely uneven surface at a 120° angle.

skills.description_en: The Read Runes skill enables a character to decipher and use magical runes (spells inscribed in suitable media). If the level of the sSpell on the rune is less than or equal to the level of the character attempting to use it to cast a spell, deciphering the rune is sufficient to use it (subject to the normal chances of sSpell failure). If the rune is a level above the character, or of a realm of magic other than the character's realm(s), a second roll, similar to the first, must be made successfully to cast the sSpell from the rune. Runes are generally not reusable, but the rune paper is. Each character should be given one chance to decipher a rune. If he fails, he may not try again until he improves his skill rank with Read Runes.

condition_modifiers.condition_en: Realm of the spell is unknown
Realm of the spell is known
Realm of the spell differs from character

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for gleaning information from a set of tracks or spoor. Detailed information about the tracks can be obtained, such as type of creatures, age of tracks, weight of creature, speed creature moving at, back tracking, etc. This skill is used for identifying the tracks and the Tracking skill is used for spotting and following the tracks.
skills.notes_en: The difficulty of this maneuver depends on the amount of information that the character is attempting to gain. The quality of the print will also modify the maneuver roll.

routine.en: Determining size of creature (tiny, small, medium, large, or huge).

easy.en: Determining if tracks belong to a humanoid or Skllls nonhumanoid.

light.en: Determining number of creatures.

medium.en: Determining type of creature (e.g., dog, cat, Wyvern, etc.).

hard.en: Determining speed. Determining age of tracks.

very_hard.en: Determining race of humanoid.

extremely_hard.en: Determining close approximation of weight.

sheer_folly.en: Determining the frequency with which a specific type of creature uses a game trail.

absurd.en: Determining if a known individual made the tracks.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus to determine if the natural order or reality has been or is going through turmoil, ( e.g., Demonic gate opening or closing, power fluctuation which is disturbing or causing the fabric of this dimension to shift, holes in the space-time continuum, residual powerful magic, etc.). Note that this skill may be severely limited in its availability.
skills.notes_en: The further the character is from the warp in reality, the more difficult this maneuver is. If the maneuver attempts to detect the opening or closing of a warp, add the level of the sSpell required to create the warp to the Reality Awareness maneuver roll. If the sSpell level is not known, assume that opening or closing a warp requires a 20th level spell. Once a warp is open the sSpell level does not modify the maneuver roll.

routine.en: Within 10'.

absurd.en: 1 mile+.

skills.description_en: This skill provides knowledge about the geography of a specific region. Note that skill for each region must be developed separately. All characters begin with ranks in Region Lore for their own region.
skills.notes_en: This skill provides characters and players with information about a single region. This allows the character to identify landmarks and other significant features (like havens, etc.). This skill is useful for determining which location best meets certain criteria. All characters begin with a rudimentary knowledge of their home region, enough to identify major landmarks.
specialties: Any specific area within the region

routine.en: Identifying the major cities in an area.

easy.en: Identifying the most important landmarks.

light.en: Identifying the significance of medium-sized cities.

medium.en: Identifying the historical significance of an area. Identifying a location that matches a general set of criteria (e.g., Where is there a good inn?).

hard.en: Identifying small towns including their most prominent features.

very_hard.en: Identifying minor landmarks.

extremely_hard.en: Identifying a location that matches a very specific set of criteria ( e.g., Where is there an inn that has a secret chamber and a back door?).

sheer_folly.en: Identifying every feature of the GM's map, including details of such locations.

absurd.en: Knowledge of most buildings larger than an abode in the region.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for recognizing and identifying major aspects of religious doctrines. Each religion may be concentrated in and learned separately for in-depth knowledge and understanding.
skills.notes_en: This skill provides characters with knowledge of one or more religions. The GM may want to provide the player with some information prior to play. Knowledge of religion is useful in understanding motivations and beliefs, which can be heavily influenced by religion. A successful Religion maneuver might provide a character with insight into a religious leader's reaction to an event, or how to motivate a congregation into action. This skill can also be used to defend the tenets of a faith within a certain context. (If God is good, why is there evil in the world?) Note that this skill is used to defend a belief, not create it.

routine.en: Simple faith.

easy.en: Knowledge of common beliefs and rituals.

light.en: Understanding of how a person should live one's life, based on the tenets of a faith. Predicting the behavior of a fanatic or zealot.

medium.en: Resolving common religious dilemmas ( e.g., can a peaceful person defend himself?).

hard.en: Knowledge of uncommon rituals and an understanding of how the beliefs relate to one another.

very_hard.en: Intimate understanding of how the religion fits into a cultural and historical context. Predicting the behavior of a highly spiritual person.

extremely_hard.en: Resolving difficult religious dilemmas (e.g., when can a peaceful faith declare war?).

sheer_folly.en: Predicting the behavior of a devout follower. Creating a new religious doctrine that incorporates aspects of one or more other doctrines.

absurd.en: Predicting the behavior of the common follower whose decisions are only slightly modified by his beliefs.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for using multiple sources of reference to assimilate the knowledge and informa• tion necessary to solve a problem, support a creative effort, or otherwise answer questions important to the individual. It includes the skills necessary to organize a search through reference material, and collate the results. Note that skill is necessary for the creation of new sSpell lists. If appropriate materials are available, a successful maneuver using appropriate materials will give up to a +40 (twice normal subsequent maneuver bonus) modification to a related Lore maneuver.
skills.notes_en: This skill can only be used to research information that already exists. Except for sSpell lists, this skill cannot be used to generate new knowledge. Some other Science/ Analytic skill must be used for that. The difficulty of a Research maneuver depends on the difficulty of the Lore maneuver being researched. Whereas Lore skills produce results much more quickly, a good researcher need learn only one skill (provided he has access to a good reference library). When researching new spells or sSpell lists, the difficulty of the maneuver depends on the similarity of the attempt to what the researcher already knows. A significant amount of time and materials will also be necessary. (See SSpell !Aw.)

routine.en: Researching a modification to a known spell.

easy.en: Researching a sSpell that will exist on a sSpell list the character has.

light.en: Researching a sSpell from another list to be inserted into a sSpell list the character has.

medium.en: Researching a new sSpell that will exist independent of any sSpell list.

hard.en: Researching a new sSpell whose effects are very dissimilar from anything with which the researcher is familiar.

very_hard.en: Researching a sSpell list that is a modification of a known sSpell list.

extremely_hard.en: Researching a sSpell list that would be classified as a Base List for the character's profession.

sheer_folly.en: Researching a new sSpell list within the character's realm.

absurd.en: Researching a new sSpell list of a different realm.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a limit for applying one's melee OB against an opponent to one's flank or rear without turning or changing position. The OB of an attack made in this fashion cannot exceed the attacker's Reverse Stroke skill bonus. Note that this skill must be developed sepa• rately for each weapon category.
skills.notes_en: This skill is self-explanatory.

skills.description_en: A single rank is necessary to keep from constantly falling off the animal being ridden. Higher skill ranks enhance the chance of controlling the animal ( of the type for which the skill is developed). This skill must be developed separately for each different type of animal.
skills.notes_en: Note that this skill involves convincing an animal to perform a specific maneuver. The mount performs the actual moving maneuver. The difficulty of the maneuver depends on the degree to which the mount naturally wants to perform the action. Natural horse actions include walking, eating, fleeing danger, etc. A horse can accelerate up to three pace categories per turn (e.g., from walk to sprint). It can decelerate a like amount. The difficulty of such a maneuver depends on the target pace. A GM may decide that some maneuvers performed in the saddle qualify as Athletic • Gymnastic maneu• vers. For example, standing in the saddle (Tightrope Walking), riding along the side of the horse (Contortions), leaping from horse to horse Uumping and/or Acrobatics), etc. % of Activity used for Riding

routine.en: Walk.

easy.en: Canter/Run. A slow turn.

light.en: Gallop (Sprint or faster). Walking up an incline or stairs.

medium.en: Trot/Jog. Jumping a low obstacle. Convincing a carnivore to attack.

hard.en: Crossing water.

very_hard.en: Jumping an obstacle the height of the mount's chest. A controlled rear. Charging.

extremely_hard.en: Approaching an obvious danger ( e.g., fire, combat, etc.). Convincing an herbivore to attack.

sheer_folly.en: Swimming.

absurd.en: Approaching an awesome danger ( e.g., a Dragon or Demon).

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for knot recognition, knottying, braiding, rope splicing, or throwing a line.
skills.notes_en: This skill covers any maneuver attempted with a rope that does not involve the character moving. Such maneuvers are covered by Climbing or Acrobatics. The skill is useful for securing prisoners or tying a boat to a dock.

routine.en: Tying shoes or boots.

easy.en: Braiding three ropes together.

light.en: Securing an inanimate object.

medium.en: Tying two ropes together to increase their length without decreasing the amount of load that can be suspended.

hard.en: Securing a person or creature (provided they are unconscious or otherwise subdued).

very_hard.en: Attaching two ropes of different sizes. Splicing two ropes together.

extremely_hard.en: Securing an animate target using a lasso ( the target gets an RR: level vs. number of ranks of Rope Mastery).

sheer_folly.en: Attaching two cables to one another.

absurd.en: Securing an animate target using a chain.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for the propulsion of watercraft through the use of paddles, oars, or poles. It combines the skills required to control and guide the craft with the coordination to work with others in rhythm (when necessary), in addition to the marshaling and pacing of internal resources to last throughout the journey.
skills.notes_en: Rowed vehicles are, in general, not particularly maneuverable, but they are more maneuverable than a sailing vehicle. It is possible to make relatively tight turns since each side of the vehicle can act inde~endently of the other. Attaining speed in a rowed vehicle is more difficult than with a sailing vehicle, however. The difficulty of a rowing maneuver is dependent on two factors: the speed of the vehicle and how sharp the maneuver is. Also, smaller vehicles (like rowboats) are more maneuverable than longships. The size of the vehicle may modify the difficulty of the maneuver by one level. The following examples assume that the vehicle is moving at a walking pace. Faster paces result in correspondingly more difficult maneuvers. Partial and Near Successes indicate that the vehicle begins to rock and might take on water. Failure indicates that the vehicle performs no maneuver and slows by one pace category. Absolute and Spectacular Failures may indicate that the vehicle tips. It takes one round to accelerate one pace category (e.g., from jog to run). These examples also assume a complete crew. Fewer rowers than normal will reduce the base speed by a similar amount. For example, a longship that requires 30 rowers has only 15. It moves at half speed. If there are fewer than one fourth of the necessary crew, the ship cannot move.

routine.en: Accelerating one pace category (e.g., from walk to jog).

easy.en: Maintaining a walk. Decelerating two pace categories.

light.en: Maintaining a jog. Making a 45 • tum.

medium.en: Maintaining a run. Making a 90 • tum. Decelerating three pace categories.

hard.en: Maintaining a sprint. Making a 135 • tum.

very_hard.en: Maintaining a fast sprint. Decelerating four pace categories.

extremely_hard.en: Making a 180 • tum.

sheer_folly.en: Maintaining a dash. Decelerating five pace categories.

absurd.en: Decelerating six pace categories.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for maneuvering a watercraft with one or more sails. It includes the skills required to control and guide such a craft as well as perform routine maintenance.
skills.notes_en: Sailing vessels are, in general, not maneuverable, but they are much faster than rowed vessels. Even when sailing into the wind, many sailing vessels can move quite rapidly. The difficulty of a Sailing maneuver is dependent on two factors: the speed of the vessel and how sharp the maneuver is. Also, smaller vessels (like river boats) are more maneuverable than galleys or long-ships. The size of the vessel may modify the difficulty of the maneuver by one level. The following examples assume that the vessel is moving at a walking pace. Faster paces result in correspondingly more difficult maneuvers. If a Sailing maneuver fails, the boat fails to maneuver; it continues in the direction it was heading the previous round, decelerating one pace category, to a minimum of a walk. Absolute and Spectacular Failures might cause the ship to be damaged or capsized. Partial and Near Successes probably indicate that the boat takes on water. The maximum attainable pace depends on whether or not the vessel is moving with the wind (dash), against the wind Uog), or perpendicular to the wind (sprint).
specialties: Any specific type of boat (e.g., clipper, yacht, etc.)

routine.en: Accelerating one pace category (e.g., from walk to jog).

easy.en: Turning up to 15 •.

light.en: Slowing down one pace category (e.g., from run to jog).

medium.en: Controlling a boat that sideswipes another ( either intentionally or not). Turning up to 30 •.

hard.en: Slowing down two pace categories (e.g., from run to stop).

very_hard.en: Controlling a vehicle that rams another (either intentionally or not). Turning up to 45 •.

extremely_hard.en: Slowing down three pace categories (e.g., from sprint to walk).

sheer_folly.en: Making a right-angle turn.

absurd.en: Slowing down four pace categories ( e.g., from sprint to stop).

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for climbing maneuvers of extended duration, including the climbing of mountains, long stairways, etc. It combines the skills required to climb safely and the marshaling and pacing of internal resources to last throughout the entire climb.
skills.notes_en: There are two major factors that determine the difficulty of a Scaling maneuver: the grade (angle) of the climb and the frequency of hand-holds. This skill is not as strenuous as Climbing, but ~}]f;~§;;:::::-€::::ic::Q;~~;t slower and more diffi- __,,,,..,__,,,,.,..,, cult. (This prevents characters from performing sustained climbs on the ceiling.) Add one difficulty level if the surface is sandy, two if wet, three if oily and four if icy.

routine.en: Scaling a 30 • incline.

easy.en: Climbing a long staircase.

light.en: Scaling a 45 • incline.

medium.en: Scaling a 60 • incline.

hard.en: Scaling a stone wall.

very_hard.en: Scaling a rough wall (like a cliff face).

extremely_hard.en: Scaling a 105 • incline.

sheer_folly.en: Scaling a smooth wall ( designed to have minimal hand-holds) or 120' stone surface.

absurd.en: Scaling a 120 • smooth surface or a 135 • stone surface.

skills.description_en: This skill gives proficiency in secretarial skills, including calligraphy, taking dictation and librarian skills.
skills.notes_en: The difficulty of a Scribing maneuver may depend on a number of factors: the clarity of the work being copied, the clarity of the speaker (dictation), the intricacy of the lettering being used, and the scribe's familiarity with the language.

routine.en: Copying a printed page.

easy.en: Generating a barely legible work.

light.en: Dictation from a slow, precise speaker.

medium.en: Copying a work written neatly, by hand.

hard.en: Generating a neat, clear work with crisp lettering.

very_hard.en: Dictation from a speaker who mumbles, stutters. or rephrases things frequently.

extremely_hard.en: Generating a work with ornate illumination.

sheer_folly.en: Copying a text that has been severely damaged by time and was poorly written originally.

absurd.en: Dictation in a language with which the scribe is not familiar. A phonetic representation of the words is produced.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus in being able to come up with the location of a particular needed item, part or ingredient. This skill is normally used in an urban type of setting. This does not cover locating food or water.
skills.notes_en: This skill is generally used to locate items that have been discarded (and are therefore free) or are illegal. In either case. the difficulty of the maneuver depends on the rarity of the item. If scrounging for a discarded item, the item's value must be taken into consideration; if scrounging for an illegal item, the penalty for selling the item is a key factor. In some cases, the GM may require a character to make a Scrounging maneuver in order to locate a part or component (since most dealers located using Contacting are purveyors of new merchandise).
specialties: Any type of item (e.g., clothing, drugs, etc.)

routine.en: Locating an item that is frequently discarded ( e.g., clothing).

easy.en: Locating a specific common item or part.

light.en: Locating an illegal good that is common and for , which the penalty for possession is minor.

medium.en: Locating a discarded item with a minor resale value. Locating an uncommon item or part.

hard.en: Locating a discarded item of little or no value.

very_hard.en: Locating an illegal good that must be smuggled into the area at some risk.

extremely_hard.en: Locating a discarded item of moderate value. Locating a specific rare item or part.

sheer_folly.en: Locating an illegal good, possession of which is dangerous.

absurd.en: Locating an illegal good that is closely guarded (e.g., military technology).

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for the manipulation of a malleable material or the carving of a resilient material into a desired three dimensional shape. This skill does not confer the ability to work materials normally unworkable (e.g., the individual must have the means to mold and form copper if the sculpture is to be formed of that metal). Note that this skill may also be used to identify styles of sculpture and to imitate other sculptures.
skills.notes_en: The sculptor's goal is the representation of a real object, scene or person. This representation is usually realistic, but may be symbolic or emotive. The difficulty of the maneuver depends on the beauty and depth of expression of the piece of art to be produced. As a sculptor gains skill, he is able to incorporate greater detail. Some sculptors allow the stone or wood to express itself naturally; the sculptor is not sure of the final product until it is completed. The perceived greatness of the work depends to a large extent on the audience. Without a rich sponsor, a sculptor leads a meager existence.
specialties: Any specific medium (e.g., clay, wood, ivory)

condition_modifiers.condition_en: From memory (+ Memory stat bonus x 3)

routine.en: A simple bowl or plate.

easy.en: A crude representation that is recognizable as a person, animal, mushroom, etc.

light.en: A stylistic representation of an object. Many primitive religious items are of this quality.

medium.en: An accurate representation of a person or object, that lacks any depth or spirit.

hard.en: A precise representation that displays every aspect in stark detail.

very_hard.en: A moving sculpture that incorporates a touch of the subject's essence or spirit. Viewers gain insight into the target's nature.

extremely_hard.en: An impressive piece that fully captures nuances in the subject's nature. The artist's relation to the subject is subtly incorporated.

sheer_folly.en: Designing a piece that inspires a new style. Such a work is refreshing in its approach to the subject.

absurd.en: A masterpiece that inspires fear or awe, depending on the subject matter.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for performing minor surgery, including sewing closed wounds, removing diseased or mangled limbs, bone setting and any other minor surgery operation.
skills.notes_en: See Healing, Section

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus when attempting to emotionally, sensually, or sexually manipulate someone. Note that this is not limited to sexual seduction! Manipulation of a person's sensual sensibilities, urging a person to indulge in some fantasy or desire, and convincing a person of some tenet on emotional rather than rational grounds can all be classified as seduction.
skills.notes_en: Seduction is used to convince the target to succumb to his desires. If the target does not have a specific desire, no amount of seduction will change his mind. Unfortunately, there are very few people who do not have any base desires; fewer still who can repress these desires when faced with temptation. The difficulty of a Seduction maneuver depends on the strength of the desire and the seriousness of the action. Some people cannot withstand the slightest temptation, while others can resist almost anything.
specialties: Any specific temptation (e.g., sex, power, money, fame, etc.)

routine.en: Convincing someone to do something he was probably going to do anyway. For example, enticing a john.

easy.en: Swaying a target to your side in a conflict, when that target is unsure of his own position.

light.en: Causing a target to fulfill a strong desire with no chance of discovery.

medium.en: Convincing someone to succumb to a normal desire when there is little risk or chance of discovery.

hard.en: Swaying a target to your side, provided the target has little stake in the outcome.

very_hard.en: Convincing a target to succumb to a normal desire when there is a significant chance of discovery. This includes any political figure whose reputation is at stake.

extremely_hard.en: Swaying a target to your side, when = the target has an investment in the outcome ( e.g., his family is in danger, he has deep philosophical beliefs, etc.).

sheer_folly.en: Seducing a target with little interest in the offer.

absurd.en: Convincing a target to do something that is dangerous or lethal. Some cults seduce their members into suicide pacts.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus to detect animate traps, ambushes or assassination in progress. Note that this is a passive skill, and should not be used when the player has indicated that he is actively searching for signs of ambush or assassins. Range is 10' per skill rank. The existence of this as an acquirable skill indicates one's ability to train one's level of sensitivity to such things, but more active observation should be handled by the skills of Observation and/or Situational Awareness.
skills.notes_en: This skill provides a penalty to Ambush maneuvers. This skill can also be used to detect traps as they are being triggered. If the result of the maneuver is some level of success, the character may be able to react before it's too late.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus to the individual's ability to discern detail and information through the use of a particular sense (i.e., sight, touch, etc.). Note that skill for each sense must be developed separately.
skills.notes_en: Sense Awareness is used automatically to detect features of the environment. The difficulty of this maneuver depends on the size or strength of the stimulus in relation to background stimuli. A Dragon on the horizon is more noticeable than a pickpocket in a crowd. Even a Dragon cannot be seen, however, if it is far enough away. Guidelines are provided for sight and sound. Other senses can be extrapolated from the examples given. Sight: An average human can spot a single man-sized figure at a distance of 500 yards. Below are the average distances at which various objects can be discerned. Details cannot be discerned until the object is one category closer. Each factor modifies the maneuver by ±10: Object Size Average Distance to Discern Minute ......................................................... 10 feet Tiny ........................................................... 100 feet Small ....................................................... 100 yards Man-Sized ............................................. 500 yards Large ..................................................... 1000 yards Huge Creature ............................................. 1 mile Small Dwelling ........................................... 1 mile Medium Dwelling .................................... 3 miles Huge Dwelling .......................................... 5 miles For example, spotting a Dragon at a range of one half mile gets a bonus of +10. Noticing a minute detail at 30' gets a penalty of -20. If the object is moving, there is an additional bonus of +10. Sound: An average human can discern a normal conversation from 50 feet away. Below are the average distances at which various volumes can be discerned. Details cannot be discerned until the object is one category closer. Each factor modifies the maneuver by Type of Sound Average Distance to Discern Very Quiet Sound ........................................ 5 feet Whisper ....................................................... 10 feet Normal Conversation ............................... 50 feet Yelling ...................................................... 50 yards Trumpet or Drum ............................... 200 yards Gun-shot ................................................ 500 yards Small Airplane ............................................ 1 mile

routine.en: Sight: On top of a mountain. Sound: In complete silence.

easy.en: Sight: On an empty plain. Sound: In a quiet alley.

light.en: Sight: On a deserted city street. Sound: While walking normally.

medium.en: Sight: On a plain with occasional trees. Sound: While moving in metal armor.

hard.en: Sight: At twilight. Sound: During a conversation.

very_hard.en: Sight: In light fog. Sound: During a storm.

extremely_hard.en: Sight: In a forest or swamp. Sound: During melee.

sheer_folly.en: Sight: In dense fog. Sound: During a rock concert.

absurd.en: Sight: On a moonless night. Sound: While a Dragon is roaring nearby.

skills.description_en: This skill gives a bonus to performing service duties, such as those of a valet, maid, butler, etc.
skills.notes_en: The difficulty of a service maneuver depends on the demeanor of the person being served. Cheerful people are much easier to please than are cranky codgers. Note that a maneuver is made only if the server is interested in pleasing the target. Otherwise, treat the maneuver as an automatic failure.
specialties: Any specific duty (e.g., retail, food service, etc.)

routine.en: Serving an optimist.

easy.en: · Serving a cheerful person.

light.en: Serving someone having a bad day.

medium.en: Serving an average person, such that the person is pleased with the service.

hard.en: Serving someone having a really, really bad day.

very_hard.en: Serving a dour or cranky person.

extremely_hard.en: Serving a pessimist.

sheer_folly.en: Serving a condescending noble.

absurd.en: Serving a Demon.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for setting traps. Traps can be lethal or non-lethal. The traps used by this skill are either very simple snares or prebuilt mechanisms that are set into place.
skills.notes_en: The difficulty of this maneuver depends on the amount of effort involved in hiding the trigger. The difficulty of this maneuver determines the difficulty of the Detect Traps maneuver needed to discover the trap.
specialties: Any specific type of trap (e.g., poison needles, pits, weapon traps, etc.) In a specific environment ( e.g., forest, treasure room, etc.)

routine.en: Traps triggered by a rope strung across a corridor.

easy.en: Traps triggered by pressure plates (e.g., a bear trap or mine).

light.en: Traps triggered by weight (e.g., a pit trap).

medium.en: Traps triggered by turning a door knob or opening a latch.

hard.en: Traps whose effect spans a large area (e.g., a collapsing wall or flooding room) unless the trigger is listed above.

very_hard.en: Gas traps.

extremely_hard.en: Traps whose trigger is on the other side of a door.

sheer_folly.en: Traps whose trigger is specific to a given individual.

absurd.en: Traps whose trigger is specific to a given individual and well removed from the trap.

skills.description_en: This provides a bonus to static maneuvers involving sewing or weaving. These skills include fashioning garments, blankets, etc. as well as repairing such items.
skills.notes_en: The difficulty of a Sewing/Weaving maneuver depends on the intricacy of the final product. The durability of the garment also modifies the difficulty of the maneuver.

routine.en: Replacing a button.

easy.en: Darning a sock.

light.en: Sewing a familiar, simple pattern ( e.g., a tunic or surcoat).

medium.en: Sewing a familiar pattern ( e.g., a shirt or jacket).

hard.en: Sewing a familiar, complex pattern (e.g., a formal gown or ceremonial garb).

very_hard.en: Sewing an unfamiliar pattern. Designing a new pattern (e.g., a shirt that contains a hidden pouch).

extremely_hard.en: Designing a complex pattern (e.g., a ceremonial robe whose encumbering pieces can easily be removed by ripping them off, for example to facilitate fighting).

sheer_folly.en: Sewing with unconventional materials (e.g., metal, plastic, etc.).

absurd.en: Designing clothing for a creature that has never been seen by the designer.

skills.description_en: Allows the user knowledge of conducting all aspects of a siege. Includes proper and best positioning of siege weapons, determining weaker portions of a wall (for siege weapon assault and/or sapping), basic engineering knowledge for building ramps, digging trenches and horse traps (for opposing cavalry), etc.
skills.notes_en: The difficulty of this maneuver depends on the quality of the structure under siege. Successful siege engineering will (over time) result in the destruction of the building's defenses. Each successful week of siege engineering reduces the DB of the structure by 5. If the DB is ever reduced to -50, the building is demolished. A successful siege engineering maneuver can also prevent attempts to re-supply.

routine.en: A straw hut.

easy.en: A wooden dwelling.

light.en: A stone dwelling.

medium.en: An outpost or wooden palisade.

hard.en: A palace.

very_hard.en: A city wall.

extremely_hard.en: A fortress.

sheer_folly.en: A heavily fortified keep.

absurd.en: A complex built out of a mountain (rather than

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus when using any form of signal communications. This skill is a specialized form of linguistics and linguistics guidelines apply.
skills.notes_en: Treat this skill as a language, subject to the following modifications: 1) Halve the number of skill ranks when referencing the Language Rank Table T-4.7 (RMFRP, p. 115). 2) Both the sender and the receiver must make successful maneuvers. If either fail, the information is garbled. Neither can temporarily provide the other with additional ranks. 3) Like Language, each form of Signaling must be developed separately.

skills.description_en: This skill is used in conjunction with an Ambush maneuver. A successful Ambush maneuver followed by a successful static maneuver using this skill (0% activity) result in the target being unable to make a sound during the attack. Note that this skill also requires that the attacker approach the foe undetected and strike before he can react. If an instant kill or immobilization result is obtained on a critical chart, the target does not make a noise.
skills.notes_en: The difficulty of a Silent Attack maneuver depends on how armored the attacker and victim are. It is also facilitated by a cushioned surface.

routine.en: Neither party is wearing armor; the floor has pillows or thick carpeting.

easy.en: The attacker is wearing leather armor.

light.en: The victim is wearing leather armor.

medium.en: Both the attacker and victim are wearing leather armor.

hard.en: The attacker is wearing metal armor.

very_hard.en: The victim is wearing chain armor.

extremely_hard.en: The victim is wearing plate armor.

sheer_folly.en: Both the attacker and victim are wearing metal armor.

absurd.en: The victim is a construct or other automaton.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for vocally reproducing musical tunes and/ or words. Note that although one may perform a song in an unfamiliar language, this skill does not provide any understanding of the language; this skill merely allows the memorization and reproduction of a song.
skills.notes_en: A singer attempts to reproduce, faithfully and emotionally, a piece of music. The intensity of the emotion and musical complexity of the song determines the difficulty of the maneuver. The impact of the performance is limited by the greatness of the composition, although a talented singer can increase the impact of even a simple song. Each Partial Success reduces the resulting fame by 20%, each Near Success, by 10%. An Absolute Success increases the fame by 20%. (See Section This skill must be developed separately for each style of music to be learned. Examples include classical, baroque, romantic, jazz, rock, etc.

condition_modifiers.condition_en: Per rank in spoken language below 4

routine.en: A scale.

easy.en: A simple melody or air. A jingle or ditty.

light.en: A piece with limited appeal. Music with technical flair, but no emotion.

medium.en: A good piece of music. The audience is entertained, but not moved.

hard.en: A piece that appeals to those who enjoy the particular style. A single emotion or idea can be communicated.

very_hard.en: A performance with broad appeal. Complex emotions or multiple ideas can be conveyed.

extremely_hard.en: A performance that will be considered great many years from now. A masterpiece that confffl) veys an entire story or emotional interaction.

sheer_folly.en: A performance that reinterprets a masterpiece.

absurd.en: A performance that gains immediate recognition world-wide.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus to the individual's ability to notice and assimilate information from the environment in a specific type of situation. This is a combination of active and passive skills specifically trained for maximum effectiveness within a given situation. For example, Scouting Awareness is used by the point man in a squad of soldiers to be alert to possible enemy approaches, traps; Sleep Awareness might aid in sleeping lightly to prevent being taken unawares; Urban Awareness might aid in staying alert for muggers, oncoming vehicles, etc. Skill with Situational Awareness must be developed for each situation as determined by the GM.
skills.notes_en: This skill is used primarily for orientation rolls and to avoid surprise. When in a potential surprise situation, each character should make a Situational Awareness maneuver. The total of the maneuver roll indicates the amount of activity that character has to use during the round of surprise. The difficulty is generally Medium. An orientation roll is required whenever a situation changes dramatically ( e.g., following a teleport, the arrival of a new combatant, etc.). The total of the maneuver roll indicates what percentage of activity the character can devote to a reaction. His remaining activity must assume that no change has occurred. The difficulty depends on how dramatic the change was. Note that it is reasonable for both sides of an encounter to be surprised by the other. Note also that a character who fails this maneuver can only act during the deliberate phase. Near and Partial Success indicates that the character can act during the normal phase. A complete Success allows the character to act during any phase.

routine.en: A trivial change occurs (e.g., a new combatant joins a fray).

easy.en: Avoiding surprise while traveling at a slow pace.

light.en: A minor change occurs (e.g., one's opponent becomes stunned).

medium.en: Avoiding surprise.

hard.en: A major change occurs ( e.g., one side or the other flees). After a Leaving sSpell to a place you can see.

very_hard.en: Avoiding surprise while traveling at a fast pace. After a Leng Door sSpell to a place you can see.

extremely_hard.en: A huge change occurs (e.g., the ground is ripped in two).

sheer_folly.en: After a Leng Door or Leaving sSpell to a place you cannot see.

absurd.en: After being teleported.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for Skating maneuvers. This may also (at GM's discretion) be used to help one stay upright on slippery or icy surfaces without skating equipment.
skills.notes_en: This skill is used to perform moving maneuvers while on skates. If the character's goal is artistic, the Dancing skill should be used, modified by the character's Skating maneuver. The difficulty of a Skating maneuver depends on the speed of the skater and the sharpness of the maneuver. Each 180° of rotation while maneuvering adds one to the difficulty level. For example, a triple rotation adds six to the difficulty. A character can decelerate one pace category per round or accelerate two pace categories without a maneuver roll.

routine.en: Decelerating two pace categories.

easy.en: Accelerating 3 pace categories. Turning 45 °.

light.en: Decelerating 3 pace categories. Skating backwards.

medium.en: Accelerating four pace categories. Turning 90°.

hard.en: Decelerating four pace categories. Maintaining balance after colliding with another skater.

very_hard.en: Accelerating 5 pace categories. Dodging a small object.

extremely_hard.en: Decelerating 5 pace categories. Dodging a man-sized object.

sheer_folly.en: Accelerating 6 pace categories.

absurd.en: Decelerating 6 pace categories.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for skiing maneuvers. This may also (at GM's discretion) be used to help one stay on one's feet when sliding down an incline without skiing equipment.
skills.notes_en: The goal of a skier is navigating a slope without falling. Whereas this sounds simple, it can be quite difficult when the slope is steep or uneven, or when the skier is trying to slow down or turn. Accelerating is not difficult, but the maximum pace depends on the grade of the slope.

routine.en: Turning 30 °. Skiing a flat surface.

easy.en: Decelerating one pace category. Skiing a smooth slope.

light.en: Turning 60°. Remaining standing following a short jump.

medium.en: Decelerating two pace categories. Skiing a rough slope.

hard.en: Turning 7 5 °. Remaining standing following a medium jump.

very_hard.en: Decelerating three pace categories. Skiing an extremely uneven surface ( e.g., a mogul hill). fffl) SICIU

extremely_hard.en: Turning 90°. Skiing an extremely steep slope.

sheer_folly.en: Decelerating four pace categories. Remaining standing following a long jump.

absurd.en: Turning 135 °. Skiing a nearly vertical surface.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for removing, preparing, treating and caring for animal skins. Note that while this would allow a simple drape to be fashioned, this does not provide the sew• ing skills necessary to create normal garments.
skills.notes_en: This skill is used either to remove a skin in order to tum it into leather or to remove a pelt intact for direct sale. The difficulty of the former depends on the toughness of the hide. The difficulty of a Skinning maneuver depends on the frailty of the hide and the amount of the pelt to be preserved.

routine.en: Obtaining an intact tail.

easy.en: Skinning a cow or pig.

light.en: Obtaining an intact pelt from a poisoned animal.

medium.en: Skinning a deer.

hard.en: Obtaining an intact pelt that includes the head.

very_hard.en: Skinning an alligator or snake. Ext. Hard: Obtaining an intact pelt ready for stuffing.

sheer_folly.en: Skinning a Dragon.

absurd.en: Obtaining an intact Dragon pelt.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for decreasing the amount of time one needs to fully recover. The sleep time is increased/ decreased by the amount the static maneuver roll is over 100 as a percentage times the racial sleep requirement. Physical prodding allows one to make a static maneuver to leave the trance, as does a painful strike (modify by +25).
skills.notes_en: This skill does not use the normal static maneuver resolution procedure. Instead, the maneuver is successful if the result of the maneuver roll is above 100 and fails (i.e., the character requires extra sleep) if the maneuver is below 100. Note that Elves usually sleep by using this skill.

routine.en: An isolated and sound-proof room.

easy.en: A quiet room in which all of the occupants are sleeping.

light.en: A still setting with some noise, but no activity ( e.g., in a peaceful glen).

medium.en: A relatively sedate setting in which there is some ac• tivity, but it is muffled. A normal wilderness setting.

hard.en: While in a room where two people are having a conversation.

very_hard.en: An active setting in which there is a great deal of movement.

extremely_hard.en: In a noisy or disruptive environment (e.g., during an argument).

sheer_folly.en: In an extremely noisy or distracting environment ( e.g., while being bombed or shot at).

absurd.en: While in melee.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for allowing one to be aware of his surroundings without the aid of sight. Blindness accrues a -100 modification to all maneuvers; Spatial Location Awareness provides a bonus to offset that penalty (the adjusted modification may actually rise as high as +10 through the use of this skill). This skill is usually used by the blind. If a non-blinded individual possesses this skill, they must reorient to use this skill (requires minimum of one round). If the individual fails this roll, he is treated as blinded for that round. Each round that the individual uses 80% of his activity to concentrate, he receives a special modification of +5 to his Spatial Location Awareness skill bonus. For each continuous, uninterrupted round of concentration, this modification increases by +5 (to a maximum of +50). The use of this skill is blocked by intervening barriers.
skills.notes_en: This skill can be used to sense objects at a distance of 2' per skill rank. There are no other modifications to the skill; it is used only to offset the penalty for blindness. The GM may still decide that some skills cannot be performed while blind ( e.g., Stone Evaluation).

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus to recognize and understand a spell. When a character realizes that he has been targeted by a spell, this skill enables him to recognize the sSpell being used against him. When attempting to recognize an unfamiliar written spell, the character must be able to read the language used.
skills.notes_en: This skill requires 100% activity the round it is used. A character can reduce this to a minimum of 60% by taking a penalty of -1 for every percentage point below 100%. This skill cannot be used to read runes, although it can be used to assist a character in deciphering a magical tome or libram. The realm of the sSpell determines the difficulty of the maneuver. Use of this skill assumes that two rounds were spent preparing the spell. If more or less time is spent preparing the spell, the difficulty of the maneuver is modified by one difficulty level for every round of difference. Instantaneous spells have a straight -30 mod.

easy.en: The spell's realm is the same as the realm of the character making the SSpell Lore maneuver.

medium.en: Essence sSpell (not same realm as maneuverer).

hard.en: Channeling sSpell (not same realm as maneuverer).

very_hard.en: Mentalism sSpell (not same realm as maneuverer). Ext. Hard: Hybrid sSpell (not same realm as maneuverer).

sheer_folly.en: Arcane sSpell (not same realm as maneuverer).

skills.description_en: This skill allows the user to modify spells within or beyond basic parameters. Each individual sSpell list requires separate skill development (as with Riding or Weapon skills). This skill might be used to change the orientation upon arrival of the target of a Teleport spell, or it might be used to allow a sSpell user to attempt to alter a bolt sSpell into a ball spell. The character should use the Power Manipulation Static Maneuver Table SM- 7.17 (p. 141) to resolve SSpell Mastery maneuvers. The result may indicate success or failure, as well as provide a modifier to any related SSpell Casting static maneuvers.
skills.notes_en: This skill is often used to increase the range, duration or area of effect of a spell, although there are a number of different uses. This skill can be used to incorporate a visual illusion into the spell. (For example, a Shock Bolt with the head of an eagle or Demon.) It can be used to tweak the effects of a spell. (For example, modifying a bolt so that it strikes the target directly, without arcing from the caster to the target.) This skill can also be used to add new parameters to a spell. (For example, a wall that can slowly advance.) SSpell Mastery can be used to lessen the effects of a spell, or to improve the caster's control over the spell's force. The possibilities are limited only by the sSpell caster's imagination (and the GM). The difficulty of the maneuver depends on how dramatic the change is. In addition, the following modifiers may also be applied: Change to Parameter
specialties: Any specific modification to a spell

condition_modifiers.condition_en: x2 range, duration, area of effect, or damage
x3 range, duration, area of effect, or damage
x4 range, duration, area of effect, or damage
x5 range, duration, area of effect, or damage

routine.en: Changing a minor aspect of a sSpell (e.g., turning a Shock Bolt green or purple).

easy.en: Adding a minor illusion or sensation (e.g., causing discomfort with a Grow or Shrink spell).

light.en: Adding a large illusion ( e.g., adding illusionary wings to a Fly spell).

medium.en: Lessening the effect of a sSpell ( e.g., using a Fire Bolt to light a campfire without igniting the surrounding woods).

hard.en: Adding a parameter with random effects ( e.g., a Wall whose height changes every round).

very_hard.en: Fine control of a sSpell ( e.g .. using a Fire Bolt to light a pipe).

extremely_hard.en: Adding a minor new parameter ( e.g., a Wall that can move 10' / round if the caster concentrates).

sheer_folly.en: Minute control of a sSpell (e.g., using a Fire Bolt to shave).

absurd.en: Adding a radical new parameter ( e.g., casting an Absolution sSpell that does not destroy the target's soul, but traps it in a glass bottle).

skills.exhaustion_point_cost: 1 every 2 rounds (1 every 6 rnds if concentrating to maintain a spell)
skills.description_en: A character's skill rank for a sSpell list determines which spells on that list he may cast. In addition, if certain restrictions are not met, a character's skill bonus for a spell's list can affect the casting of that spell. A character learns spells by developing skill ranks for the sSpell list that the sSpell is on. A character knows all of the spells on a list that have a level less than or equal to his skill rank for that sSpell list. Normally, a character may cast any sSpell on a list that has a sSpell level that is less than or equal to his skill rank for that list (i.e., any sSpell he knows). A character must use Power Points to cast a spell.

This skill provides a bonus for maximizing one's speed over short distances, including marshaling and pacing one's resources (exhaustion point expenditure) and running in a safe and non-injurious manner.
skills.notes_en: Using this skill to maneuver on a flat, smooth surface (like a race-track) is detailed in Sec. 16.0 of RMFRP (p. 51). Unfortunately, characters rarely find themselves on ideal running surfaces. The following examples assume that a character is dashing with a weight penalty of -1 to -10 (normally a Medium maneuver). Adjust other situations accordingly.

medium.en: Open road.

hard.en: Trail or open plains.

very_hard.en: Rocky or broken ground, forest.

extremely_hard.en: Soft ground (e.g., snow, sand, etc.).

sheer_folly.en: Up a 30° slope.

absurd.en: Up a 45 • slope, steeper slopes require Climbing.

skills.description_en: Stalking is the ability to move using silence, camouflage, and shadows to conceal your presence; Hiding is the same ability used when not moving.
skills.notes_en: This skill involves moving from one location to another without being seen or heard. A character attempting to stalk should make two maneuver rolls, one for sound and one for sight. Should either fail, the thief has been spotted. In some situations, only one maneuver roll may be necessary ( e.g., approaching a target from behind, whil~ silenced, while invisible, etc.). The difficulty of remaining unseen should be referenced under Hiding. The difficulty of remaining silent depends on how armored the thief is, and how noisy the ground is. This maneuver roll should be modified by double the Missile Attack Penalty (RMFRP, p. 215) of the armor worn.
specialties: Any specific environment ( e.g., castles, forest, etc.)

routine.en: Stalking across pillows or thick carpeting.

easy.en: Stalking over moss.

light.en: Stalking across thin carpeting.

medium.en: Stalking across a wooden floor.

hard.en: Stalking through leaves.

very_hard.en: Stalking through underbrush.

extremely_hard.en: Stalking across squeaky floorboards or gravel.

sheer_folly.en: Stalking while carrying a large bag of coins, or other noisy object.

absurd.en: Stalking while carrying a living being ( e.g., another person, an animal, etc.).

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for determining dates, directions, and locations when the stars are visible. May provide a bonus to one's Navigation skill.
skills.notes_en: This skill is commonly used to provide a subsequent maneuver bonus to Navigation or Orienteering. In addi• tion, this skill can be used to recognize constellations, tell time, or provide a bonus to Astrology. This skill cannot be used if there is significant cloud cover, and does not provide nearly the detail provided by Astronomy.

routine.en: Recognizing major constellations.

easy.en: Recognizing major stars and planets.

light.en: Identifying a specific direction.

medium.en: Recognizing nearly all constellations.

hard.en: Determining the current time.

very_hard.en: Recognizing minor stars.

extremely_hard.en: Determining one's location on the planet.

sheer_folly.en: Knowledge of when to expect meteor showers, comets, etc.

absurd.en: Identifying all named stars.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for maneuvering on stilts or other extensions to one's height.
skills.notes_en: The difficulty of walking on stilts depends on the height of the stilts (as a ratio of the character's height). Divide the height of the stilts by the character's height and reference the result below. If the ground is uneven or rocky, the maneuver may be modified by up to two difficulty levels.

routine.en: 1" heels.

easy.en: 1" - 3" heels.

light.en: 3" - 6 11 extensions.

medium.en: 0.25x character's height.

hard.en: 0.5x character's height.

very_hard.en: 0.75x character's height. SKll.l

extremely_hard.en: 1.0x character's height. t!ffl

sheer_folly.en: 2.0x character's height.

absurd.en: 4.0x character's height.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for recognizing and identifying stones. Also will give information on famous, magical, powerful, historic, legendary or cursed types of stone. Skill is developed separately for each culture or region as applicable to the GM's world scheme.
skills.notes_en: The skill provides the character with knowledge about the stones found in a specific region. Unlike many Lore skills, the GM will probably not be able to provide the player with information prior to play. The rarity of a given stone must first be determined. Common rocks (like sandstone or clay) probably have a rarity of 1. Precious stones have a higher rarity rating, magical and enchanted stones, the highest rarity rating. See Section 5.8 (p. 17) for more information about Lore maneuvers.

routine.en: Identifying a precious stone.

easy.en: Identifying a sedimentary stone.

light.en: Identifying a metamorphic or igneous stone.

medium.en: Determining the purity of a stone.

hard.en: Determining the relative strength of a mineral.

very_hard.en: Determining if a stone has any flaws (e.g., stress fractures, impurities, etc.).

extremely_hard.en: Identifying a stone from another area of the world.

sheer_folly.en: Knowledge of any magical or supernatural properties inherent to a stone.

absurd.en: Precise information about every known use for a stone.

skills.description_en: This skill provides a bonus for working with stone and creating sculptures, writings, or any other form of inscription or relief.
skills.notes_en: The difficulty of stone-working depends on the intricacy of the final product. Even a large, bulky product might require very detailed workmanship in order to function properly (e.g., an ornate carving). Like many Crafts skills, this skill assumes that the craftsman has an adequate supply of tools. Without the necessary implements, there is little a craftsman can do. (Increase all maneuvers by at least 3 difficulty levels.)

routine.en: Making a crude object (e.g., a brick).

easy.en: Making an object that requires a single stone, and no detailed crafting ( e.g., quarrying).

light.en: Cutting a stone into a shape with nonright angles (e.g., a triangle).

medium.en: Building a simple stone wall.

hard.en: Cutting a gem stone into a basic shape (this does not add any value to the stone).

very_hard.en: Building a structure that has many arches, domes or other challenging portions.

extremely_hard.en: Cutting a gem stone into an exquisite shape in order to increase its value.

sheer_folly.en: Building an architect's masterpiece that includes dramatic curves and careful attention to detail.

absurd.en: Cutting a magical or enchanted gem stone (like laen).

skills.description_en: Awareness of events and protocol in the street society and underworld and the ability to interact with the individuals who run and live in the common orounderworld society. Note that this skill does not necessarily enable a character to find such individuals.
skills.notes_en: This skill is used to win the trust of a criminal or underworld society. It includes knowing the slang, who to hate and who to respect. A successful maneuver is required when a character interacts with a person who has legitimate reason to fear the law. If the maneuver is not successful, the criminal will not interact with the character for fear of discovery. Law enforcement officials need this skill to infiltrate criminal organizations. Once a character is a member of a given organization, he need make a maneuver roll only when interacting with other organizations, or with the leaders of his own organization. The difficulty of the maneuver depends on how much the criminal has at stake. (The guildmaster of the assassin's guild probably doesn't trust anyone.) The GM may adjust the difficulty of the maneuver if the character has been particularly faithful to the organization or individual.
specialties: Any specific organization or specific city

routine.en: The criminal is a petty thief or pick-pocket.

easy.en: The criminal is a street thug.

light.en: The criminal is a member of an organized gang.

medium.en: The criminal is part of a formal criminal organization.

hard.en: The criminal has been busted recently.

very_hard.en: The criminal runs his own organization, or is guilty of one or more serious offenses (like murder or rape).

extremely_hard.en: The local authorities are actively pursuing the criminal.

sheer_folly.en: The criminal runs a large organization with multiple investments and rackets.

absurd.en: The criminal is insane.

skills.description_en: This skill represents the basic and most simple forms of a formal "hard" martial art. These forms must be learned before an individual may progress to the higher degrees, as it represents the fundamentals of the art. Criticals should be rolled on the Martial Arts Strikes Critical Strike Table 4.5 (AL, p. 98).
skills.notes_en: See Martial Arts Companion.
specialties: While facing a specific style or weapon

skills.description_en: This skill represents the intermediate forms of a "hard" martial art. Note that this skill is more effective than the Striking Degree 1 skill. Because this skill builds on the previous degree of this art, the ranks of this skill cannot exceed the ranks the character has in Striking Degree 1. Criticals should be rolled on the Martial Arts Strikes Critical Strike Table 4.5 (AL, p. 98).
skills.notes_en: See Martial Arts Companion.
specialties: While facing a specific style or weapon

skills.description_en: This skill represents the advanced forms of a "hard" martial art. Note that this skill is more effective than the Striking Degree 2 skill. Because this skill builds on the previous degree of this art, the ranks of this skill cannot exceed the ranks the character has in Striking Degree 2. Criticals should be rolled on the Martial Arts Strikes Critical Strike Table 4.5 (AL, p. 98).

This skill represents the most advanced forms of a "hard" martial art. Note that this skill is more effective than the Striking Degree 3 skill. Because this skill builds on the previous degree of this art, the ranks of this skill cannot exceed the ranks the character has in Striking Degree 3. Criticals should be rolled on the Martial Arts Strikes Critical Strike Table 4.5 (AL, p. 98).

This skill enables an individual to throw off the effects of stunning blows. A successful static maneuver using this skill will remove one or more rounds of stun from the maneuverer. Failure may result in greater disorientation than before the use of this skill.
skills.notes_en: This skill can be attempted once per round. If the result of the maneuver roll is at least Near Success, the character may act that round. Additional rounds of stun are removed from the end (i.e., a character must make a Stun Removal maneuver each round until there are no rounds of accrued stun.) Maneuver Result Change Spectacular Failure ......................... +3 rounds of stun Absolute Failure .............................. +2 rounds of stun Failure or Unusual Failure .............. +1 round of stun Partial Success ................................................. No effect Near Success ....................................... -1 round of stun Success ................................................ -2 rounds of stun Absolute Success .............................. -3 rounds of stun Unusual Success ............................... -5 rounds of stun

medium.en: 1 round of stun.

hard.en: 2 rounds of stun.

very_hard.en: 3-4 rounds of stun.

extremely_hard.en: 5-7 rounds of stun.
